title: WF-015 hardening (2/3) — audit integrity verifier covers the WHOLE log (pagination)
business_reason: The verifier checked only the most-recent 1000 events (Supabase's single-query cap), so a forgery buried deeper than 1000 rows back would be missed. Page through the entire audit log so the integrity guarantee covers the full trail.

design.pagination: GET /api/staff/security-audit/verify-chain now pages newest-first through audit_events in 1000-row .range() batches until exhausted (bounded at MAX_PAGES=100 = 100k events). Per-event integrity is order-independent, so full coverage is correct.
design.honesty: the response carries coverage: 'full' | 'partial' — if the 100-page bound is hit it reports 'partial' rather than silently truncating (no-silent-caps principle).
design.ui: the integrity panel shows 'Verified N events (full log)'.

param_14.question: Does the integrity check cover the entire audit log?
param_14.answer: Yes. The verifier pages through every audit event (newest-first, in 1000-row batches up to a 100k bound) and reports whether coverage was full or partial — so a forgery anywhere in the trail is caught, not just in the recent 1000.
param_14.security_implications: closes a coverage gap (deep forgeries previously unchecked); explicit partial-coverage signal prevents a false sense of completeness.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: a scheduled sweep (item 3) so full verification runs automatically; streaming verify for very large logs beyond 100k.

verify: npx playwright test audit_integrity (asserts coverage=full + checked>1000)
